measurement: SG aim-circle, core, and boss-silhouette screen geometry, extracted from owner-drawn overlays on full-resolution frames (2026-07-22)
status: HYPOTHESIS-SUPPORTING MEASUREMENT + FORMAL FIT - winning model (see formalFit_16cell): pellets UNIFORM per area in a screen-fixed aim circle that shrinks ~39% at Hit Rate 38.91, landing = silhouette coverage x ~0.96, core = range-scaled core disc area ratio. Passes a no-free-parameter landing check and the 16-cell fit with one outlier cell (midfar ON core). NOTHING ENACTED.

method.summary: The owner draws overlays on native-resolution (2622x1206) screenshots (magenta outline = boss silhouette, cyan circle = core / grey aim circle); the session extracts them by color-threshold, flood-fills the silhouette, and circle-fits the rings. This is the method of record for boss/circle geometry: direct segmentation of the un-annotated frames was tried earlier and is INFEASIBLE (dark mech on grey fog).
method.semantics: Owner: 'everything inside the pink line is the boss (not including the line)'; 'inside and not including the blue circle' for core and grey-circle tracings. Radii therefore use the stroke's INNER edge (5th-percentile radial distance); ring-fit pen slop is ~6 px.
method.script: docs/probes/drawn-geometry/extract_drawn_geometry.py (cv2; debug overlays verified visually before any number was trusted)

inputs.role: clean frame, near band (clock 02:16), boss in owner-chosen near-neutral pose
SAME frame + magenta boss outline + cyan core circle
brid-silent-track (SG) solo, near band (clock 02:06): cyan tracing of the grey aim circle at Hit Rate 0
soda-twinkling-bunny (SG) control fight, near band in a Full Burst window (clock 02:14): cyan tracing of the grey aim circle at Hit Rate 38.91

ownerRulingsThisSession: The 8-cell band assignments of soda-tb-sg-core-hr-windows.json are CONFIRMED correct (owner hand-measured) - candidate (d) band-misassignment is DEAD.
Boss on-screen size at a given band is the same across recordings at the same resolution; only limb animation varies, and the traced frames were chosen near a neutral pose.

Pellet impacts are visually weighted toward the OUTER EDGE of the grey circle, arriving in groups of 2-3 along the rim - not center-clustered (owner observation from footage). The formal fit honors this WITHOUT an extra edge exponent: per-area-uniform already puts 75% of pellets beyond half the circle radius; what the observation genuinely rules out is the center-clustered Gaussian.

Boss shape: four legs, two large shoulders/arms, narrow hourglass waist, core at the waist center - so as the boss shrinks with range, MORE wide hittable structure enters the fixed circle, holding coverage roughly flat.

The grey circle is the SAME across all shotgun users at every Hit Rate (owner, already tested elsewhere) - the cross-unit HR contrast (brid-silent-track HR0 vs soda-twinkling-bunny HR38.91) is therefore clean, and the same-unit re-trace strengthener is RETIRED.

core_px.note: diameter ~32 px matches the older near-band '31 px' core figure, implying the old 31->17 px core-size series were DIAMETERS

silhouette_near.note: flood-filled from the outline incl. pen-crossing pockets (~5k px)

derived.circleShrinkAtHr3891: 79.3 -> 48.2 px = 39% shrink, weapon-matched (both SG). The live cone's SG sigma-shrink (0.009/HR => 35% at 38.91) is essentially the CIRCLE shrink applied to the wrong object; the earlier '9-25% measured' figure was an artifact of the now-dead centered-Gaussian premise.

landingCheck_noFreeParameters.model: landing = coverage of the HR-state circle by the traced silhouette scaled per band by px ~ 1/distance (band distances 20.7/30.7/40.7/50.7 from the effective-range readout), circle centered on the core
landingCheck_noFreeParameters.verdict: 6 of 8 cells within ~0.03 with ZERO fitted parameters; the 'impossibly flat' landing column is reproduced. The near band over-predicts in both arms (candidates: loose outline, aim wander, popup dropout).

formalFit_16cell.model: radial pellet density ~ r^k in the HR-state circle; landing = density-weighted silhouette coverage x efficiency eps; core = (r_core/R)^(k+1) conditional on landing; core hitbox screen-fixed vs range-scaled compared by AIC; binomial likelihood over 8 landing + 8 core cells

runnerUp.note: the interim 'screen-fixed core / k~2 ring' reading from the first pass does NOT survive the formal fit (deltaAIC 10.5)

formalFit_16cell.reading: k~1 = UNIFORM per area in the circle. The owner's 'pellets grouped along the outer edge' is exactly what per-area-uniform looks like radially (75% of a disc's area lies beyond half its radius) - no extra edge exponent needed. eps~0.96 = ~4% landing loss (dropout / aim wander).
formalFit_16cell.cellQuality: all 8 landing cells |z|<1.9; core cells fine except ONE outlier: midfar ON measured 0.0786 vs predicted 0.0354 (z=+2.76, 11 cores where ~5 expected). Deviance 24.8 on 14 dof (GOF p~0.04, marginal - traced-radius pen slop is not in the error model, and the midfar ON core cell alone contributes ~7.6).

derived.resolvedPuzzles: This dissolves the flat-landing anomaly of the 2026-07-22 SG geometry handoff section 6 (the non-convex silhouette keeps filling the fixed circle), and the mid-cell core rate sits at z=-1.4 (unremarkable). The one residual outlier is the MIDFAR ON core cell (z=+2.76) - open item.

openItems: MIDFAR ON core outlier (z=+2.76, 11 cores where ~5 expected) - the one cell the winning model misses. Owner will count a midfar Hit-Rate-ON window from a DIFFERENT video (independent sample beats a recount). Pre-registered predictions for that count: core/landed ~0.035 if the model is right vs ~0.08 if the outlier is real; landing ~0.73-0.78. Needs: SG unit, midfar band, HR buff ON in-window, ideally >=10 shots, section-2 counting rule of the handoff.
eps~0.96 landing loss not yet attributed (loose outline vs aim wander vs popup dropout).
Burst 2 near-ON window (video 47-62) recount stays open as a pure reproducibility backstop.

Independent radial-distribution check - PILOT SUCCEEDED (2026-07-22): during soda-twinkling-bunny shots the aim disc renders discrete RED capsule-shaped marks inside it (plus small white rings at/beyond the rim), both crisply machine-detectable; Hough-fitting the disc edge finds R=48.5-48.6 px on three independent frames, cross-validating the owner's traced 48.2 px by an independent method. OPEN before scaling: what the red marks encode (per-pellet landing positions vs some other indicator) - validate by tracking marks across one shot's frames in burst 5 (video 162-177) and comparing counts against ownerCount.shots; owner may simply know what they are.
Any engine change from this is a gated /scientific-method pass - not this session.
